idea.title: Beast Bazaar
idea.hook: Two cute critters touch and POP into a chonkier beast — and there's always a customer who wants the next size up. Merge your way up the species ladder before the bazaar gridlocks.
idea.prototype: Merge-board generator economy: tap a nest to spawn tier-1 critters, merge two same-tier into the next tier, fulfil escalating customer orders for coins, spend coins in a tiny shop; endless score-chase, lose by board gridlock.
idea.mutation_of: merge-board generator economy (Merge Dale / Gossip Harbor form)
idea.why_novel: our portfolio's merge games are all hybrids (merge_conquest=battler, merge_guns=shooter, merge_cleaners=cleaning) and soviet_suika covers drop-physics — a pure generator→merge→fulfill-orders→coins economy is untested for us, and it is the dominant grossing merge form with a high retention ceiling
idea.lose_condition: board FULL (every cell occupied, freeCells()===0) = GRIDLOCK — free-move merges + a 30-cell board with only 11 tiers make a full-board-with-no-merge state unreachable by pigeonhole, so board-full itself is the deliberate, reachable terminal pressure (merge/deliver to keep space open)

sources.title: Merge Dale
sources.snippet: #1 puzzle_objects CTR archetype on Yandex today (weighted score 18429, 3.4x the next) — generator+merge board economy

sources.title: Gossip Harbor: Merge & Story
sources.snippet: 50M+ installs, heat 0.86, tight 8-app merge-&-story grossing cluster — validated monetisable merge-economy mechanic

synthesizer_brief: Signal+knowledge convergent pick for 2026-05-31 am run: merge is the #1 CTR archetype, dominates Play-Store grossing (Gossip Harbor/Travel Town/Flambé cluster), is explicitly on the high-retention-ceiling genre list, and the generator-economy form is novel for our portfolio. Scoped to an in-SESSION 10-minute loop only (retention deferred to iteration).

alternatives.title: Chonk Pit
alternatives.hook: Suika-style physics drop-merge of cute critters

alternatives.title: Conveyor Critters
alternatives.why_novel: cleaning/sort family (our 94%-liked winner) but lower retention ceiling than merge-economy

alternatives.title: Royal Decorate
alternatives.why_novel: too art-heavy to ship in one session
